2606.16817.title: Understanding the Behaviors of Environment-aware Information Retrieval
2606.16817.paper_summary: Recent retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) approaches have demonstrated strong capability in handling complex queries, yet current research overlooks a critical challenge: different retrievers require fundamentally different query formulation strategies for optimal performance. In this work, we present the first systematic analysis of how LLMs can learn to adapt their query formulation strategies for different retrievers via reinforcement learning (RL). Our empirical study reveals that RL effectively teaches an LLM to tailor its queries to specific retriever characteristics. We discover that different retrievers exhibit surprisingly distinct optimal query styles (e.g., descriptive vs. question-like), suggesting strategies learned for one retriever ineffective for another. We further show that performance can be enhanced by incorporating retriever-specific human guidance and by scaling model size. To facilitate learning over multi-retrieval-step trajectories, we introduce a branching-based rollout technique that improves training stability. Our work provides the first empirical evidence and actionable insights for building truly retriever-aware RAG systems. Code and resources are available at https://github.com/LCO-Embedding/Envs-aware-Information-Retrieval.
2606.16817.paper_summary_zh: 最近的檢索增強生成（RAG）方法已顯示出在處理複雜查詢方面的強大能力，但目前的研究忽視了一個關鍵挑戰：不同的檢索器需要根本不同的查詢表達策略以達到最佳性能。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們首次系統性地分析了大型語言模型（LLMs）如何通過強化學習（RL）學習調整其查詢表達策略以適應不同的檢索器。<lb/>我們的實證研究顯示，強化學習有效地教會LLM根據特定檢索器的特徵來定制其查詢。<lb/>我們發現不同的檢索器展現出驚人地不同的最佳查詢風格（例如，描述性與類似問題的），這表明為一個檢索器學習的策略對另一個檢索器可能無效。<lb/>我們進一步顯示，通過融入檢索器特定的人類指導和擴大模型規模，可以提升性能。<lb/>為了促進在多檢索步驟軌跡上的學習，我們引入了一種基於分支的回滾技術，這改善了訓練的穩定性。<lb/>我們的工作提供了首個實證證據和可行的見解，以建立真正意識到檢索器的RAG系統。<lb/>代碼和資源可在 https://github.com/LCO-Embedding/Envs-aware-Information-Retrieval 獲得。
2606.16817.author: Ruifeng Yuan et.al.
2606.16817.authors: Ruifeng Yuan, Chaohao Yuan, David Dai, Yu Rong, Hong Cheng, Hou Pong Chan, Chenghao Xiao
